instruction: Classify the security alert into a threat category.
baseline_prompt: You are a SOC alert triage analyst. Classify the security alert into a threat category. Classify into one of these categories: phishing, malware, brute_force, data_exfiltration, insider_threat, ddos.
source: Authored for TEI-Bench (Sonnet-generated, Sonnet-validated; kept only label-agreed examples).

train.query: Received alert on email with spoofed display name matching our CFO sent to accounts payable team. Message requests updating vendor payment account details urgently. Analysis shows DKIM and SPF authentication failures, and the actual sender domain is newly registered overseas.

Network sandbox detected self-propagating worm behavior from host 192.168.50.143 scanning internal subnet for SMB vulnerabilities. Automated exploitation attempts targeting ports 445 and 139 across 2,847 internal IP addresses in past 15 minutes. Payload delivery observed via ADMIN$ share writes. Conficker-like lateral movement patterns identified.

FTP server on 172.16.8.99 experienced 890 failed login attempts from host ftp-scanner.suspicious-domain.ru targeting anonymous and default accounts. Logs indicate automated tool systematically testing common credential pairs against port 21.

Web proxy alerts indicate marketing manager's account accessed personal Dropbox 23 times today, uploading 890 files totaling 4.7 GB. Historical baseline shows this user typically uploads fewer than 5 files monthly. Content inspection suggests documents contain proprietary product roadmaps and pricing strategies.

Terminated employee badge used to access secure server room 40 minutes after exit interview completed. Physical access logs show 18-minute duration inside facility and multiple failed login attempts to decommissioned equipment.
SNMP trap received from border router indicating sustained packet rate of 4.2 million PPS. Traffic analysis reveals SYN-ACK reflection attack exploiting misconfigured cloud instances.

User accessed a website from an email link that cloned our company intranet login page pixel-perfect. The URL uses HTTPS but the certificate is self-signed. Browser isolation sandbox captured the page submitting entered credentials via POST request to a command-and-control server in Eastern Europe.

VirusTotal submission of suspicious PDF document returned 47/68 detection ratio. Document exploits CVE-2013-3346 buffer overflow vulnerability in Adobe Reader to execute shellcode. Drops second-stage payload disguised as Windows Update component. Traffic analysis reveals communication with Darkcomet RAT infrastructure.

VPN concentrator logs indicate sustained authentication failures against multiple employee accounts. Single source IP 45.142.212.98 attempted to authenticate 412 times using various username/password combinations over 2-hour period. Zero successful sessions established.

Network traffic analysis shows sales laptop established encrypted tunnel to remote server using non-standard port 8443, followed by sustained 600 Mbps outbound throughput for 90 minutes. Timing coincides with access to customer database containing contact lists and contract terms. Employee submitted resignation yesterday.

System administrator created three new privileged accounts with domain admin rights using naming convention that bypasses standard approval workflow. Accounts were created at 11:32 PM and activity logged from administrator's home IP address.

Web application firewall blocking massive influx of malformed HTTP requests. 450,000 connections attempted in 5 minutes from residential proxy network. Origin server unresponsive to legitimate traffic.

Suspicious email detected with IRS branding threatening legal action for unpaid taxes. Message pressures recipient to click a link and verify identity immediately to avoid penalties. The link analysis shows it leads to a credential harvesting page collecting full personal and financial information under the guise of tax verification.

Suspicious DLL injection detected in process memory space of explorer.exe on host 10.45.23.199. Unknown module loaded from %APPDATA%\Roaming\msupdate.dll exhibiting keylogging behavior and clipboard monitoring. Code signing certificate invalid. Behavioral analysis shows credential harvesting patterns consistent with Emotet trojan family.

API gateway monitoring detected abnormal pattern: 3,200 authentication requests to /api/v2/auth endpoint from distributed botnet. Each request contained different API key combinations. All returned 401 Unauthorized responses. Rate limiting has been applied.

test.query: Firewall logs show an employee workstation established 47 consecutive HTTPS connections to a newly registered domain in the past hour, transferring approximately 2.3 GB of data to an external IP address located in a non-business region. The destination server has no prior communication history with our organization.

Finance department user executed 23 failed attempts to access restricted merger and acquisition folders on the executive file server within a 10-minute window, despite having no M&A project assignments.
Database connection pool exhausted due to 15,000 simultaneous TCP connection attempts from distributed sources. Application tier reporting complete service outage for past 8 minutes.

Email security system quarantined messages claiming to be password expiration notices from IT. The emails contain QR codes that, when scanned, direct mobile devices to fraudulent authentication pages designed to harvest both passwords and MFA codes in real-time.

Windows Defender ATP identified WannaCry indicators on workstation DESK-7742. Ransomware variant detected encrypting network shares with .wcry extension. SMB exploitation via EternalBlue vulnerability CVE-2017-0144 confirmed. Bitcoin wallet demand note dropped in multiple directories. System isolated from network pending remediation.

SSH service on production server 10.45.23.12 logged 89 failed authentication attempts from 203.0.113.58 in the last hour. Username enumeration pattern detected with common names like root, admin, user, test being cycled through systematically.

Database audit logs show contractor credentials executed query extracting 45,000 customer records including credit card information at 11:47 PM. Same session established external database connection to unknown MySQL server and performed bulk INSERT operations. Contractor access was scheduled to expire next week.

Employee account jsmith accessed 847 customer records from the CRM database outside normal business hours at 2:47 AM on a Sunday, with no associated trouble ticket or business justification in the access logs.
CDN provider reports volumetric attack reaching 45 Gbps directed at our primary domain. Traffic analysis shows UDP flood from botnet with geographically distributed origins across 67 countries.

User reported suspicious email with Amazon branding stating their account was compromised. The message creates urgency by claiming unauthorized purchases were made. Investigation shows the embedded link uses URL obfuscation techniques and redirects through multiple domains before reaching a fake login form.

Antivirus quarantined Trojan.GenericKD.62518433 from email attachment invoice_March2024.zip. Extracted payload attempted to download secondary stage from compromised WordPress site. PowerShell obfuscation detected with base64-encoded shellcode. User jsmith@company.com clicked attachment before automated blocking engaged.

Database server alert: PostgreSQL authentication failures spiked to 2,300+ attempts in 20 minutes from IP range 91.200.12.0/24. Connection attempts used dictionary-based username list against default port 5432. All attempts rejected due to invalid credentials.

Endpoint detection identified suspicious PowerShell script execution on finance department laptop that enumerated shared drives, compressed sensitive files into password-protected ZIP archives, and initiated FTP transfer to external server in Eastern Europe. User was on vacation during this activity.

Receptionist account showing SQL injection patterns against internal HR portal attempting to elevate privileges and access performance review database. Account created search queries for termination records and executive compensation data.
IDS signature match: NTP amplification attack detected with reflection factor of 556x. External DNS servers being abused to flood our edge routers with 38 Gbps of spoofed response traffic.

Multiple employees forwarded an email claiming to be from our benefits provider with subject line about health insurance enrollment deadline. The sender address uses our company name but a different TLD. Clicking the enrollment link presents a form requesting employee ID, SSN, and banking information.

Mobile device management flagged Android application com.systemupdate.core sideloaded outside Google Play containing Anubis banking trojan. Application requests dangerous permissions including SMS intercept and accessibility services. Network traffic shows exfiltration of contact list and installed app inventory to command server in Bulgaria.

Multiple authentication failures detected from IP 185.220.101.47 targeting user account 'admin' over the past 15 minutes. System logs show 247 consecutive failed login attempts with varying password combinations. Account has not been locked out yet.

SIEM correlation rule triggered on anomalous pattern: HR system access followed by bulk export of employee records, subsequent login to personal webmail, and large outbound SMTP traffic. Investigation reveals attachment contained spreadsheet with salary information and social security numbers sent to non-company domain.

Network logs indicate contractor account querying Active Directory for all VPN user credentials and exporting results to CSV file. Contractor role is limited to help desk password resets with no authorization for bulk credential access.
Load balancer threshold exceeded: 12,500 requests per second hitting API endpoint /checkout from 890 IP addresses. Pattern indicates coordinated HTTP GET flood. Service degradation confirmed.

Employee clicked on a link in an email claiming to be from DocuSign requesting signature on an urgent contract. The link led to a fake Microsoft 365 login page. Web proxy logs show the destination domain was registered 3 days ago and uses a typosquatted version of the legitimate service.

Host-based IDS triggered on polymorphic code execution in memory region of chrome.exe. No corresponding disk-based file found indicating fileless attack vector. Process hollowing technique identified injecting malicious code into legitimate browser process. Beacon traffic to 203.0.113.88 every 60 seconds matches Cobalt Strike patterns.

Cloud infrastructure console showing repeated failed authentication events for IAM user 'backup-service'. Logs indicate 178 unsuccessful login attempts with incorrect passwords from unfamiliar geographic location. MFA was not completed for any attempt.
